title: oxalis luteola
intro: Oxalis luteola, also known as the yellow wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which help to eliminate parasites, reduce inflammation, and increase urine production. The health benefits of Oxalis luteola include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Oxalis luteola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often consumed in small doses to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
